documentation.description:
This package describes an interaface that the panel can used to control
a simulator.  Specifying this interface should allow easier implementation
of multiple simulator.

Basic types for address and data bus.  Currently these support up to 32
bits of address and data.  These will need to change if larger processors
are to be supported.

simple_types.name: addr_type
simple_types.code: type addr_type is (ADDR_NONE, ADDR_INTR, ADDR_DATA, ADDR_INST, ADDR_IO);

documentation.description: Control and mode switches and LEDs

simple_types.name: dev_type
simple_types.code: type dev_type is (NL, TT, FD, HD, CL, PT, MT);

documentation.description:
The actual interface.  These are routines that are called under specific
circumstances.  They can examine the switch register to further decide
their actions and set the LED registers as desired.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Simulator control

Called first to initialize the simulator

simple_types.name: proc_mode
simple_types.code: type proc_mode is (PROC_NONE, PROC_KERN, PROC_EXEC, PROC_SUP, PROC_USER);

array_types.name: page
array_types.code: type page is array (0 .. 255) of byte;

documentation.description: Processor modes

record_types.name: ctrl_mode

fields.name: auto
fields.description: Auto/Man, enable remote control via web server

tagged_types.name: io_device
tagged_types.code: type io_device is abstract tagged limited private;

documentation.description:
Codes for various device types
NL - Null device
TT - Serial interface/terminal
FD - Floppy disk
HD - Hard disk
CL - Clock
PT - Paper tape
MT - Magnetic tape

tagged_types.name: simulator
tagged_types.code: type simulator is abstract tagged private;

documentation.description: The I/O device object for simulated CPUs

access_types.name: io_access
access_types.code: type io_access is access all io_device'Class;

documentation.description:
Codes for various device types
NL - Null device
TT - Serial interface/terminal
FD - Floppy disk
HD - Hard disk
CL - Clock
PT - Paper tape
MT - Magnetic tape

access_types.name: sim_access
access_types.code: type sim_access is access all simulator'Class;

documentation.description: The I/O device object for simulated CPUs
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Memory page for reading various hex formats
Set and clear breakpoints.  The implementation is up to the specific simulator.

documentation.description:
Simulator object.

  This just contains the switch and light registers for interfacing with
  a control panel.  Each specific simulator will have to add its own data.

documentation.description:
Called to start simulator execution at a specific address.  This is made
  null rather than abstract so that simulators that don't use it don't need
  to override it.

documentation.description:
Called once per frame when start/stop is in the start position and run/pause
  is in the run position.

Trace levels are handled by the implementation.  The only globally defined
